page: Amazon Web Services d3en.12xlarge is a dense storage instance optimized for network and EBS performance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores operating at 3.1 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. A key feature of this instance is its massive local storage capacity, offering 335,520 GB of HDD storage, alongside a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the d3en.12xlarge in the average performance tier for most compute and memory tasks. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score is 40,652.97, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 10,414.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 108.27 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 48.07 GB/sec for large block write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 23.51 GB/sec, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at an average level of 62.35 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the d3en.12xlarge provides high storage density per resource unit, making it a cost-effective choice for workloads requiring massive local capacity without the premium cost of SSDs. However, the reliance on HDD local storage represents a performance tradeoff for random I/O operations compared to NVMe-backed instances. This instance is highly suited for storage-heavy workloads, including large-scale data lakes, distributed file systems, big data analytics, and high-capacity backup or archiving systems.
description: Amazon Web Services d3en.12xlarge is a dense storage instance designed for high-capacity data workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at 3.1 GHz, it features 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a massive 335,520 GB of local HDD storage. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 40,652.97 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10,414.0. Given its massive HDD capacity and balanced compute-to-memory ratio, the d3en.12xlarge is highly optimized for storage-intensive applications, distributed file systems, data warehousing, and large-scale log processing where sequential throughput and storage density are prioritized over random I/O speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.12xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, 335.5 TB of local HDD storage, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth for massive data workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d3en.12xlarge is a dense storage instance with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 335.5 TB of local HDD storage.
tagline: A dense storage instance offering massive local HDD capacity and high network bandwidth for data-intensive and distributed file system workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor
Massive 335,520 GB of local HDD storage for high-density data requirements
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for data transfer
Best suited for distributed file systems, data lakes, and large-scale archiving
